title: Maine Lobster Roll New England

seo.title: Classic Maine Lobster Roll Recipe - New England Style
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Maine Lobster Roll at home with our authentic New England recipe. Fresh lobster, buttery bun, and simple ingredients for a taste of the coast!
longTailKeywords: how to make Maine Lobster Roll
authentic Maine Lobster Roll New England style
best Maine Lobster Roll recipe at home
lowCompetitionKeywords: New England style lobster roll recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Maine Lobster Roll Recipe (New England Style)
h2Headings: What is a Maine Lobster Roll?
Ingredients for the Perfect Maine Lobster Roll
Step-by-Step: How to Make Maine Lobster Rolls
Tips for the Best Maine Lobster Roll
Variations: Hot vs. Cold Lobster Rolls
seo.imageAlt: A classic Maine Lobster Roll with fresh lobster meat, mayonnaise, and chives, served on a toasted bun.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the iconic taste of New England with this homemade Maine Lobster Roll recipe – perfect for summer!

description: Experience the quintessential taste of summer with this classic Maine Lobster Roll, a beloved New England delicacy. Succulent, sweet lobster meat is lightly dressed and nestled into a perfectly buttered and toasted bun. This recipe brings the authentic coastal flavor right to your kitchen, perfect for a special meal or backyard gathering.

ingredients.notes: from 2-3 lobsters, chilled, cut into 1-inch pieces
good quality, such as Hellmann's or Duke's

instructions.title: Prepare the Lobster Meat
instructions.text: If starting with whole lobsters, steam or boil them until cooked through, about 8-10 minutes for 1.25 lb lobsters. Allow them to cool completely, then remove the meat from the tails, claws, and knuckles. Roughly chop the lobster meat into 1-inch pieces and ensure it is thoroughly chilled before proceeding.

instructions.title: Make the Lobster Dressing
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the mayonnaise, finely diced celery, fresh lemon juice, chopped chives, salt, and black pepper. Whisk gently until all ingredients are well combined and the dressing is smooth.

instructions.title: Combine Lobster with Dressing
instructions.text: Add the chilled chopped lobster meat to the bowl with the dressing. Gently fold the lobster into the mixture, ensuring all the pieces are lightly coated. Be careful not to overmix, as this can break up the delicate lobster meat.

instructions.title: Chill the Lobster Salad
instructions.text: Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the lobster salad for at least 15-20 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld together and ensures the roll is served refreshingly cold, which is characteristic of the Maine style.

instructions.title: Prepare the Buns
instructions.text: Brush the outsides of the top-split hot dog buns generously with melted butter. Heat a large skillet or griddle over medium heat. Place the buttered buns flat-side down on the hot skillet and toast for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and lightly crispy.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: Carefully open each toasted bun. Fill each bun generously with the chilled lobster salad. Garnish with a sprinkle of fresh chives, if desired. Serve immediately with a side of potato chips, coleslaw, or a pickle spear for a complete New England experience.

tips: For the best flavor and texture, always use fresh, high-quality lobster meat. If using frozen, thaw it slowly in the refrigerator.
Do not overmix the lobster salad; a gentle fold ensures the delicate lobster chunks remain intact and succulent.
Toast the buns properly in butter until golden brown; this creates a crucial textural contrast and adds rich flavor to the roll.
article: Maine Lobster Roll New England is a beloved American Seafood dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
